zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Childe Baron de Torpel於1085年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾時，他的父親Roger Childe Baron de Torpel30歲，母親Baroness Roger de Torpel30歲。他和Madam Robert Childe至少有3個兒子。他去世於1147年之後在英國諾薩普頓史爾比特爾波洛，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾索可 本 比特爾波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Childe Baron de Torpel于1085年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾时，他的父亲Roger Childe Baron de Torpel30岁，母亲Baroness Roger de Torpel30岁。他和Madam Robert Childe至少有3个儿子。他去世于1147年之后在英国諾薩普頓史爾比特爾波洛，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾索可 本 比特爾波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Torpel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans Peter, Kurt, Ulrich.
German:
nickname from Middle High German top(p)el ‘game of dice’.

variants.name: Topel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Fritz, Gerd, Heinz.
German (Töpel): habitational name from Töpeln near Leipzig or Töppeln near Gera, probably both deriving their names from Slavic topol ‘poplar’.
German: from Old Slavic toplъ ‘warm’, possibly applied as a nickname. Compare Toppel .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Childe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Germanic names formed with hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Hilda
meanings: Of Germanic origin, a Latinized short form of any of several girls' names derived from hild ‘battle’. Many of these are found in both Continental Germanic and Old English forms. St Hilda ( 614–80 ) was a Northumbrian princess who founded the abbey at Whitby and became its abbess. The name all but died out by the 14th century. It was strongly revived in the 19th century. Since the 1930s, however, it has again fallen from favour.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Childe Baron de Torpel was born in 1085, in Northamptonshire, England, his father, Roger Childe Baron de Torpel, was 30 and his mother, Baroness Roger de Torpel, was 30. He had at least 3 sons with Madam Robert Childe. He died after 1147, in Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England, and was buried in Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Childe Baron de Torpel est né en 1085, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger Childe Baron de Torpel, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Baroness Roger de Torpel, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Madam Robert Childe. Il est décédé après 1147, à Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Childe Baron de Torpel 1085 in Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Childe Baron de Torpel 30 und seine Mutter Baroness Roger de Torpel 30. Er hatte mit Madam Robert Childe mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb nach 1147 in Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Childe Baron de Torpel è nato il nel 1085 nel Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Roger Childe Baron de Torpel, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Roger de Torpel, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Madam Robert Childe. Morì dopo il 1147, nel Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England, e fu sepolto a Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Childe Baron de Torpelが1085年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Roger Childe Baron de Torpelは30歳，母Baroness Roger de Torpelは30歳であった。彼はMadam Robert Childeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1147年以後にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアペテルボロウで死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアソク オフ ペテルボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Childe Baron de Torpel(이)가 1085년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Childe Baron de Torpel(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Roger de Torpel(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Madam Robert Childe(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1147년 이후 영국 노르섬프토실르 피터버루그h에서 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 소크 프 피터버루그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Childe Baron de Torpel nasceu em 1085, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Childe Baron de Torpel, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Roger de Torpel, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Madam Robert Childe. Ele faleceu após 1147, em Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Childe Baron de Torpel родился в 1085 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Childe Baron de Torpel было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness Roger de Torpel, 30. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Madam Robert Childe. Он умер после 1147 г. в местности питирбороую, нортамптоншир, Англия и был похоронен в местности сок оф питирбороую, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Childe Baron de Torpel nació en 1085, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Childe Baron de Torpel, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Baroness Roger de Torpel, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Madam Robert Childe. Murió después de 1147, en Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Soke of Peterborough, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
